toolUseResult.prompt:
Update parity spec files to reflect features completed in Batch 5 and 6 of the Revit parity push.

**Files to update:**

1. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/revit-parity/ch04-walls.md`
2. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/revit-parity/ch11-floors-parapet.md`
3. `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/revit-parity/ch03-levels-views.md`

Read each file carefully before editing. Only change the features listed below.

---

**ch04-walls.md updates:**

F-035 (Wall Types / Type Selector): currently `🟡 Partial — bim-ai has wall type data structures but no rich type-selector UI with a library of pre-built types.`
Update to ✅:
`**bim-ai status:** ✅ Available — \`workspace/OptionsBar.tsx\` now renders a "Type:" \`<select>\` dropdown when the wall tool is active, listing all \`wall_type\` elements from \`elementsById\`. The selected wall type ID is stored in \`useBimStore.activeWallTypeId\` and passed as \`wallTypeId\` to \`createWall\` in \`PlanCanvas.tsx\`. Missing from Revit: a rich type dialog with visual preview and built-in types library.`

F-039 (Wall Top Offset / Bottom Offset): currently shows `🟡 Partial — bim-ai supports wall height and base/top level constraints in the data model but not via interactive Options Bar controls.`
Update to ✅:
`**bim-ai status:** ✅ Available — \`InspectorContent.tsx\` (wall \`case\`) now shows editable "Base Offset (mm)" and "Top Offset (mm)" number inputs (step 50, \`defaultValue\` keyed by element ID). On blur they fire \`onPropertyChange?.('baseConstraintOffsetMm', val)\` and \`onPropertyChange?.('topConstraintOffsetMm', val)\`, which triggers \`updateElementProperty\` in \`WorkspaceRightRail\`. The backend \`CreateWallCmd\` and element model already carry these fields.`

---

**ch11-floors-parapet.md updates:**

F-108 (Floor type selection): currently `🟡 Partial — floor type data exists in the engine but no UI type-selector for floors.`
Update to ✅:
`**bim-ai status:** ✅ Available — Two surfaces: (1) \`workspace/OptionsBar.tsx\` renders a "Type:" \`<select>\` when the floor tool is active, listing all \`floor_type\` elements; the selection is stored in \`useBimStore.activeFloorTypeId\` and passed via \`SketchCanvas.floorTypeId\` → \`finishSketchSession opts.options.floorTypeId\` → the Python emitter (which already handles \`floorTypeId\` in opts at line 267 of \`sketch_session.py\`). (2) The wall inspector already had a floor type selector when a floor element is selected (in \`InspectorContent.tsx\` \`case 'floor':\`).`

F-110 (Wall Top Offset — negative for sub-slab): currently `❌ Not available — bim-ai has no top/bottom offset controls.`
Update to ✅ or 🟡:
`**bim-ai status:** ✅ Available — \`InspectorContent.tsx\` (wall \`case\`) now has editable "Base Offset (mm)" and "Top Offset (mm)" fields supporting negative values (step 50). Negative base offset (e.g. −200) moves the wall base below the level datum, enabling the sub-slab placement pattern shown in Revit.`

---

**ch03-levels-views.md updates:**

F-025 (Levels): currently `🟡 Partial — bim-ai has a \`datum_levels.py\` module supporting level data, but level heads, symbol visibility in elevation views, and the plan-view auto-generation are not implemented in the frontend.`
Update to 🟡 (improved):
`**bim-ai status:** 🟡 Partial — \`LevelStack.tsx\` now shows a "+" button in the header that fires \`createLevel { id, name, elevationMm: maxElev + 3000, alsoCreatePlanView: true }\`, automatically creating both the level and a companion plan view. Elevation is editable inline for all levels. Level name can be renamed via double-click. Missing: level head symbols visible in elevation/section views; elevation datum line across the plan canvas.`

---

After editing:
- Commit on branch `feat/parity-tracker-batch5-6` with message: `docs(parity): promote F-025 F-035 F-039 F-108 F-110 (Batch 5+6 ships)`
- Push and merge to main: `git checkout main && git merge --no-ff feat/parity-tracker-batch5-6 -m "merge: parity tracker — Batch 5+6 completions" && git push origin main`

input.description: WP-VIEW-SCALE — add 1:N scale display + architectural presets (F-010)
input.prompt: Add a proper 1:N scale indicator and architectural scale presets to the plan canvas zoom control (F-010 — "View Scale selector"). **File to modify:** `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx` **Context:** - `halfUi` state (initialized at 344 as `useState(22)`) holds the camera half-width in meters; updated via `setHalfUi(camRef.current.half)` at line 637 - `plotScale = halfUi * 2` gives the N in 1:N (since `worldHalfMm = halfUi * 1000` and `plotScale = worldHalfMm / 500 = halfUi * 2`) - `zoomPresets` is defined around line 3271 with label+half pairs (in meters) - The zoom button at ~line 3446 shows a graphical scale bar + cm text **Precise changes:** 1. Near line 3270, after `const sb = THREE.MathUtils.clamp(halfUi * 0.25, 0.2, 6);`, add: ```typescript const plotScaleN = Math.round(halfUi * 2); ``` 2. Replace the `zoomPresets` definition (the 5-item array around line 3271) with architectural scale presets: ```typescript const zoomPresets = [ { label: '1:25 — detail', half: 12.5 }, { label: '1:50 — room', half: 25 }, { label: '1:100 — floor', half: 50 }, { label: '1:200 — building', half: 100 }, { label: '1:500 — site', half: 250 }, { label: '1:1000 — master', half: 500 }, ] as const; ``` 3. In the scale bar button JSX (~line 3446-3460), after the existing scale bar graphic + cm text, add the 1:N indicator. Change the button content area to include the scale ratio. Find the span that currently shows `{`${(sb * 100).toFixed(0)} cm`}` and add a sibling span after it: After `{`${(sb * 100).toFixed(0)} cm`}`, add: ```tsx 1:{plotScaleN} ``` **Notes:** - Read the file first to confirm exact line numbers and surrounding context - The `as const` on the zoomPresets array is needed for TypeScript; the new array is also `as const` - Do not change any other part of the file **After edits:** - `pnpm typecheck` — verify no new errors in this region - Commit on branch `feat/parity-view-scale` with message: `feat(plan): 1:N scale indicator + architectural zoom presets (F-010)` - Merge to main: `git checkout main && git merge --no-ff feat/parity-view-scale -m "merge: view scale indicator + presets (F-010)" && git push origin main`
